title: periploca scandens
intro: Periploca scandens, also known as poison vine, is a woody vine native to Africa and Asia, characterized by its grayish-brown bark and clusters of small, blue-purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly the presence of periplocin. The health benefits of Periploca scandens include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Periploca scandens is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
